question: [File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketTickSpacing] Can an unprivileged attacker (non-tickSpacingSetter) call setMarketTickSpacing(id, newTickSpacing) directly under the precondition that tickSpacingSetter != address(0) and the market exists, triggering the call sequence [attacker.call(setMarketTickSpacing(id, 1))], violating the invariant that only tickSpacingSetter can modify marketState[id].tickSpacing, causing scoped impact: unauthorized tick-spacing reduction unlocks previously inaccessible ticks for all offers in the market? Proof idea: fuzz caller != tickSpacingSetter and assert revert with OnlyTickSpacingSetter().

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketTickSpacing + multicall] Can an unprivileged attacker use multicall([abi.encodeCall(setMarketTickSpacing, (id, 1))]) under the precondition that multicall uses delegatecall and msg.sender is preserved, triggering the call sequence [attacker.multicall([setMarketTickSpacing(id,1)])], violating the invariant that only tickSpacingSetter can call setMarketTickSpacing, causing scoped impact: access-control bypass via multicall delegatecall context? [File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketTickSpacing + priceToTick] Can the tickSpacingSetter set newTickSpacing to a value that does not divide MAX_TICK (5820) under the precondition that newTickSpacing > 0 and marketState[id].tickSpacing % newTickSpacing == 0 but newTickSpacing does not divide 5820, triggering the call sequence [tickSpacingSetter.setMarketTickSpacing(id, newSpacing), priceToTick(price, newSpacing)], violating the invariant that priceToTick always returns a tick <= MAX_TICK, causing scoped impact: priceToTick returns (low + spacing - 1) / spacing * spacing > MAX_TICK, making tickToPrice revert with TickOutOfRange on subsequent take calls? [File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketTickSpacing + take] Can the tickSpacingSetter reduce spacing from 4 to 2 after makers have signed offers at tick 2 (previously inaccessible) under the precondition that offers at tick 2 exist off-chain and spacing was 4 at signing time, triggering the call sequence [tickSpacingSetter.setMarketTickSpacing(id, 2), taker.take(offer_at_tick_2, ...)], violating the invariant that maker intent is not filled under terms the maker did not anticipate, causing scoped impact: maker offers at newly unlocked ticks are filled without maker re-consent, potentially at unfavorable prices?
